summary: Excellent front‑of‑board capture of a solid 1980 show with superb ambience despite hand‑held clapping artifacts.
review: The reviewer praises the performance as a very good show that nearly reaches the coveted “X” factor, and rates it four‑plus stars musically. The recording, made with Jim Wise’s two shotgun Nak CM‑700s, is highlighted as technically outstanding – crisp front‑of‑board sound with great ambience, though the handheld mics pick up noticeable clapping. Overall the combination of strong playing and high‑quality capture makes this release a must‑listen.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed, five‑star review emphasizes both an excellent performance and top‑tier recording quality; the rating reflects a strong show (70%) plus outstanding technical fidelity (30%).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑board handheld shotgun mics (Nak CM‑700s) provide clear, crisp capture with superb ambience; clapping is pronounced due to hand‑held placement, and the mix includes clean reverb especially early in Jack Straw.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A very good concert that skirts the elusive “X” factor; overall energy is strong with a smooth flow, though no specific song moments are singled out by reviewers.
